hub_subtitle: Business English para Gestão de Riscos e Finanças

lesson.menu_desc: Debating an imagined risk scenario and negotiating with a partner bank, in the second conditional -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 5 -- What If: Negotiating the Hypothetical
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sergio Takashi Tanaka | Lesson 5 -- What If

listenings.text: Hello, Sergio. This is the supervisory team at the central bank, with one hypothetical for your review. Imagine a severe but unlikely scenario. If a major partner bank became insolvent overnight, what would your desk do? If that bank froze its credit line, would you have enough collateral to cover the exposure? We are not saying this will happen. We only want to know how you would react if it did. If you needed a waiver on a limit, you would ask us first, not after. Please send us your answer in writing before the review. If your plan were clear, the whole process would be much faster. Thank you.

Hi Sergio, it is Paulo from the credit desk. A quick thought before the negotiation with the partner bank. I have run the hypothetical. If they cut our credit line by half, we would still be fine, but the buffer would be tight. If they asked for more collateral, I would offer a small concession, not a big one. Honestly, if I were you, I would not accept their first offer. I would make a counteroffer with a longer deadline. If the downside stayed limited, we would sign; if it grew, we would walk away. Call me before the meeting. Thanks.

extra_audio.text: First, imagine the partner bank made a surprise move. If the bank froze our credit line, we would post more collateral the same day. If they asked for a concession, we would offer a small one, not a big one. If the market crashed at the same time, we would negotiate a waiver instead of a fire sale. Finally, if their counteroffer protected the buffer, Sergio would accept it; if it did not, he would ask for more time.
